title: Dashboard Overview - Finding Your Way Around iMash
lede: You just logged in — now let's turn that wall of tiles, tabs, and tables into a map you know by heart. In ten minutes you'll know exactly where every part of the platform lives and how to read your home page like a pro.

steps.title: Meet your home page
steps.content:
iMash packs an AI call center, a full CRM, dispatch, messaging, and marketing into one screen — and it all starts here. When you log in, you land on the Overview page.

  1. Log in to your iMash dashboard. The Overview page loads automatically.
  2. To come back here at any time, click Overview at the very top of the left sidebar.
  3. Notice the three zones of every iMash screen:
    • Left sidebar — every page of the platform, grouped by area (Intelligence Center, Messages, Call Center, CRM, and more). It scrolls, and the < arrow at the bottom collapses it when you want more room.
    • Header — the search bar, notifications, and the big section tabs you'll use to jump between areas.
    • Main area — the content of whatever page you're on. Right now, that's your account snapshot.
💡 The Overview page is read-only reporting — nothing you click here edits your data, so explore freely.

images.alt: The iMash Overview home page with sidebar, header, statistics tiles and calls table
images.caption: Your home base: left sidebar, header navigation, and the Overview snapshot of your whole account.

images.alt: The iMash Overview home page with sidebar, header, statistics tiles and calls table
images.caption: Your home base: left sidebar, header navigation, and the Overview snapshot of your whole account.

steps.title: Get to know the header toolbar
steps.content:
The card at the very top of the screen is your command strip — it follows you to every page. From left to right:

  • Search … — a global search across your contacts, leads, jobs, agents, phone numbers, calendar events, and call logs. Click it (or press /), type a name, and jump straight to the record.
  • 🔔 Notification bell — opens your in-app notification feed. You can create your own alert rules (for example, "notify me when an invoice is paid") and even enable browser push notifications from here.
  • Live: — a live counter of calls in progress right now. Click it to open the Live Calls monitor.
  • Role tag — the green (owner) or orange (sub-user) pill showing your role and login email. Click it to see exactly which permissions your account has.
  • Language — switch the dashboard language (English, Spanish, and more).
  • Theme toggle — the moon/sun switch flips the entire app between light and dark mode. Try it — every screenshot in these docs exists in both.
  • Account menu — hover over the logo icon to see your email, with Account Settings (profile, password, branding) and Logout.
  • 📞 Phone button — opens the built-in web dialer so you can place and receive calls right from your browser, on any page.

images.alt: iMash header toolbar with search, notification bell, live counter, role tag, language, theme toggle, account menu and phone button
images.caption: The header toolbar: search, notifications, live-call counter, role tag, language, theme toggle, account menu, and the web dialer.

images.alt: iMash header toolbar with search, notification bell, live counter, role tag, language, theme toggle, account menu and phone button
images.caption: The header toolbar: search, notifications, live-call counter, role tag, language, theme toggle, account menu, and the web dialer.

steps.title: Navigate with the section tabs
steps.content:
Right below the toolbar sits the section tab strip — the fastest way to move between the big areas of the platform. Click a tab and both the page and the left sidebar switch to that world. If your screen is narrow, the strip scrolls sideways — drag it to reveal the rest.

  • Automation — your Intelligence Center: build AI voice & text agents, visual automation flows, and chat in Smart Studio. Start with Create Agent and Smart Flows.
  • Contact Center — live calls, human agent groups, phone numbers, PBX extensions, SIP providers, call logs, and your knowledge base. See the Contact Center overview.
  • CRM — accounts, contacts, leads, deals, quotes, invoices, tasks, products, and your calendar. Begin with Contacts.
  • Dispatch — field-service jobs, technicians, and automatic tech dispatch. See the Dispatch overview.
  • Messages — two-way SMS, WhatsApp, and Email conversations, all in one place.
  • Campaigns — outbound AI calling campaigns, dial lists, and the human predictive dialer. Start at Create Campaign.
  • Marketing — build lead lists: import contacts, scrape businesses, and mine LinkedIn.
  • User Management — invite teammates, assign roles and permission groups, and review the audit log. See Team Management.
  • Billing — your balance, plans, payment methods, and usage. See Billing.
  • Credentials — API keys for connecting your own systems. See API Keys.
💡 Don't see one of these tabs? Your administrator may have hidden that area for your role — tabs only appear for sections you're allowed to use.

images.alt: iMash header with the section tab strip: Automation, Contact Center, CRM, Dispatch, Messages, Campaigns, User Management and more
images.caption: The section tabs, with the active section highlighted and its sub-tabs docked underneath.

images.alt: iMash header with the section tab strip: Automation, Contact Center, CRM, Dispatch, Messages, Campaigns, User Management and more
images.caption: The section tabs, with the active section highlighted and its sub-tabs docked underneath.

steps.title: Drill in with the sub-tab strip
steps.content:
When you click a section tab, a second row of smaller chips docks underneath it — the section's own pages, each with a live record count. It's a mini table of contents for wherever you are.

  1. Click CRM in the section tab strip.
  2. Look at the docked chips: Overview, Accounts, Contacts, Leads, Deals, Invoices, Quotes, Tasks, Products, Services, and more — the number under each label is how many records you have.
  3. Click any chip to open that page. The highlighted chip always tells you where you are.
  4. If there are more chips than fit, use the › arrow (or swipe) to scroll the strip.

images.alt: The CRM section selected with its sub-tab strip showing Overview, Accounts, Contacts, Leads, Deals, Invoices, Quotes and Tasks chips with record counts
images.caption: The CRM sub-tab strip: one chip per page, each with a live record count.

images.alt: The CRM section selected with its sub-tab strip showing Overview, Accounts, Contacts, Leads, Deals, Invoices, Quotes and Tasks chips with record counts
images.caption: The CRM sub-tab strip: one chip per page, each with a live record count.

steps.title: Read the Total Statistics tiles
steps.content:
Back on the Overview page, the first card is Total Statistics — all-time counts across your whole account. These numbers ignore the date filter further down; they are your account at a glance.

  • Contacts, Leads, and Accounts — how many CRM records you have.
  • Notes and Calendar entries — activity logged against those records.
  • AI agents, Campaigns, Phone numbers, and SIP providers — your calling setup.
  • Knowledge base — documents your AI agents can search during calls.
  • Sub accounts — teammates you've invited.
  • Custom tools — custom actions your agents can trigger.

If you have sub-accounts, use the All owners dropdown in the card's top-right corner to narrow every tile to a single owner.

images.alt: Total Statistics tiles showing counts for contacts, leads, accounts, notes, calendar entries, AI agents, campaigns, phone numbers, SIP providers, knowledge base, sub accounts and custom tools
images.caption: All-time totals for every kind of record in your account.

images.alt: Total Statistics tiles showing counts for contacts, leads, accounts, notes, calendar entries, AI agents, campaigns, phone numbers, SIP providers, knowledge base, sub accounts and custom tools
images.caption: All-time totals for every kind of record in your account.

steps.title: Check your call performance cards
steps.content:
Below the tiles is a strip of four performance cards. Unlike Total Statistics, these do follow the date filter — they always describe the time window you've selected.

  • Total calls — how many calls happened in the window, with the average duration underneath.
  • Total minutes — total talk time, with your success rate.
  • Total spent — what those calls cost, with the per-minute rate.
  • Avg cost / call — the average price of one call, with an estimated daily spend.
💡 These four numbers are the pulse of your AI call center. Glance at them each morning — a sudden change in success rate or cost per call is your earliest signal that something needs attention.

images.alt: Four performance cards showing Total calls, Total minutes, Total spent and Avg cost per call
images.caption: The date-scoped performance strip: calls, minutes, spend, and cost per call.

images.alt: Four performance cards showing Total calls, Total minutes, Total spent and Avg cost per call
images.caption: The date-scoped performance strip: calls, minutes, spend, and cost per call.

steps.title: Filter what you see
steps.content:
The Filters bar controls the performance cards, the Calls table, and the charts below it. The card's subtitle always shows the active window (for example, Last 30 Days · Jun 9 – Jul 9).

  1. Click the date button (it shows Last 30 Days by default) and pick Today, Yesterday, This Week, This Month, Last 30 Days, or Custom Range. Choosing Custom Range reveals a start–end date picker.
  2. Narrow by Phone numbers — only calls on a specific line.
  3. Narrow by Agent — one AI agent, or Web Dialer for calls placed by humans from the browser dialer.
  4. Narrow by Tags — pick one or more outcomes such as Voicemail, Transfer, No Answer, Agent Ended Call, or Customer Ended.
  5. If you have sub-accounts, filter by Owner to see one teammate's calls.
  6. Type a name in Search customer… to find calls with a specific person.

images.alt: The Filters bar with date range, phone numbers, agent, tags, owner and customer search controls
images.caption: The Filters bar — everything below it obeys these controls.

images.alt: The Filters bar with date range, phone numbers, agent, tags, owner and customer search controls
images.caption: The Filters bar — everything below it obeys these controls.

steps.title: Explore the Calls table
steps.content:
The Calls card lists every call that matches your filters — your account's story, one row at a time.

  • Contact / Lead — who was on the call. Click the name to open the full contact or lead record in a side drawer without leaving the page.
  • Agent / Campaign — the AI agent that handled the call (with its campaign underneath), or Web Dialer for human browser calls.
  • Date & Time — when it happened; the small arrow shows the direction (outbound ↗ in green, inbound ↙ in blue) and the number underneath is your line that carried the call.
  • Duration — call length as hh:mm:ss.
  • Type — Phone for regular calls, web for browser calls.
  • Tags — the outcome (Completed, Voicemail, No Answer…) plus tags your AI extracted; hover over the +N pill to see the rest. Scroll the table sideways to also see the Owner column.
  • Actions — the ▶ play icon for the recording and the 👁 eye icon to open the complete call log with transcript and AI analysis.
  1. Use the pagination controls at the bottom-right to page through results, change the page size, or jump straight to a page.

images.alt: The Calls table listing calls with contact, agent, date and time, duration, type, tags and action icons
images.caption: Every call in your selected window — click a row's eye icon for the full transcript.

images.alt: The Calls table listing calls with contact, agent, date and time, duration, type, tags and action icons
images.caption: Every call in your selected window — click a row's eye icon for the full transcript.

steps.title: Scan the analytics charts
steps.content:
The bottom row turns the calls on the current page into three quick charts:

  • Reason call ended — a bar per outcome (agent ended, customer ended, voicemail…). Hover over a bar for the exact count.
  • Avg duration by agent — the mean call length per AI agent, in minutes. Great for spotting an agent whose conversations run long or get cut short.
  • Call volume by hour — when your calls happen across the day, so you can schedule campaigns and staff for the peaks.

That's the whole dashboard — you now know where everything lives. Time to put something on it!

images.alt: Three analytics charts: reason call ended, average duration by agent, and call volume by hour
images.caption: The analytics row: outcomes, average duration per agent, and hourly call volume.

images.alt: Three analytics charts: reason call ended, average duration by agent, and call volume by hour
images.caption: The analytics row: outcomes, average duration per agent, and hourly call volume.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaDescription: A guided tour of the iMash dashboard: the Overview home page, the header section tabs and sub-tabs, the toolbar (search, notifications, theme, account menu), statistics tiles, performance cards, filters, the Calls table, and analytics charts.
